JS From Zero EP.13: What Is the DOM, Really?
RE：從零開始的學習 JS 生活
第 11 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

Day thirteen of my JavaScript journey. This post is all about the DOM — the Document Object Model. We'll cover what the DOM tree is, what DOM elements, text, and attributes are, and why a NodeList isn't quite an array. It's the foundational concept every web dev needs to get.

JS From Zero EP.12 — Loops: for, while, and do...while

Day twelve of being reborn into JavaScript, and this time we're talking about loops. We'll go through the three basic ones — for, while, and do...while — how they actually run, and the classic beginner trap of writing an infinite loop by forgetting to update the counter.

RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 11: Why Does 1 == '1' Return true?

Day 11 of the reincarnation. This time we dig into type coercion in JavaScript — one of the most common and most important concepts in the language. We'll talk about weak vs strong typing, why JavaScript loves to convert types behind your back, and why == can return some genuinely weird results.
/en/javascript/2019/09/25/js-from-zero-10-conditionals/
Learning JS From Zero — Day 10: if, else, and switch

Day 10 of the journey. This post is all about conditional statements — basically the if...else... family and switch. We'll cover how each one works, why an empty string counts as false, and the classic switch gotcha where forgetting break makes every case fall through.

RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 9: Logical Operators (and Why They Don't Always Return true/false)

Day 9 of getting reincarnated into JS land. Back on Day 6 we covered operators, but we skipped the logical operators — &&, ||, and !. So this time we'll fill that gap, walk through what each one does, and end with a neat catch from Kuro: these operators don't always hand you a clean true or false.
/en/javascript/2019/09/23/js-from-zero-ep08-anonymous-functions/
JS From Zero EP.8 — Anonymous Functions and IIFEs

Day 8 of getting reincarnated into JavaScript. Last time we covered functions, so this time we're looking at anonymous functions — functions with no name. We'll also dig into IIFEs (immediately invoked function expressions), why they don't pollute the global scope, and the classic loop-with-setTimeout gotcha.

Re:Learning JS From Zero — Day 7: Functions, Your Reusable Code Wrappers

Day 7 of my JS isekai. This time we're getting into functions — one of those things you absolutely have to understand to write code. We'll look at why functions exist, how to define a named function, what that mysterious event parameter is, and how wrapping repetitive code saves you from editing the same thing 100 times.

RE:Zero — Learning JS From Scratch, Day 6: Operators (and Why 1 + '1' Is '11')
RE:Zero — Learning JS From Scratch
11th iThome Ironman Contest

Day 6 of my reincarnated JS life. This time we cover operators. We've already bumped into a few of them, but here I want to walk through the common ones properly — arithmetic operators, comparison operators, loose vs strict equality, and that weird thing where 1 + '1' gives you '11' instead of 2.
/en/javascript/2019/09/20/js-from-zero-05-arrays-and-objects/
RE: Learning JS from Zero — Day 5: Arrays and Objects

Day 5 of my reincarnation into JavaScript. This time we get into arrays and objects — two of the most-used data structures in JS. We'll cover array literals, reading and changing values, object literals, dot notation vs bracket notation, and why JavaScript itself is basically just one giant object.

RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 4: Events and Event Listeners

Day 4 of my reincarnation. After getting selectors down yesterday, the next thing to learn is events and event listeners. We'll cover what an event is, how to wire up addEventListener, and the third argument nobody talks about — event bubbling vs. event capturing — plus how to stop it with stopPropagation.
